scope.dataset: Amazon-C4 plus Amazon Reviews 2023 temporal history

matched_model.selection_note: History budget and truncation were selected from label-free token-coverage audits; no dev qrels were read by training or scoring.

data_integrity.wrong_history_caveat: No query repeats exist in dev, so wrong histories use a matched global other-user donor and are weaker provenance controls than same-query exchange.

history_budget_adequacy_probe.protocol: The history budget increased from two to eight and max length from 512 to 1024 using label-free tokenizer coverage only. Complete query-plus-eight-event context is preserved; QC and FULL retain matched examples, seed, objective and optimizer updates.

h8_minus_h2.interpretation: More history makes the model substantially more directionally responsive and more user-specific, but the accompanying loss of query-candidate base capability grows even faster, so end ranking becomes significantly worse.

serialization_factorial_control.conclusion: Serialization has a significant checkpoint-dependent interaction, but it cannot explain the base gap: FULL weights remain dramatically worse than QC weights under both pure-query and structured-empty inputs. The base erosion is therefore not an empty-marker artifact.

interpretation.primary: Amazon-C4 contradicts a universal direction-failure claim: an ordinary full-token Transformer can convert semantic purchase history into strongly correct candidate-relative direction and large true-over-null utility on this constructed-query retrieval task.
interpretation.boundary_value: This is a useful positive control. It localizes the KuaiSearch pattern toward natural-query nonrepeat candidate competition rather than inability of Transformers to read or use any semantic history.
interpretation.base_degradation: The two-event personalized checkpoint falls far below the independently trained QC model when history is removed, while true history restores only parity. With eight events, true history no longer fully repairs the larger base loss. A QC/FULL by serialization factorial shows a checkpoint-dependent format interaction but a much larger FULL-weight deficit under either format, rejecting the empty-marker explanation.
interpretation.history_budget_result: The frozen eight-event adequacy probe rules out short history as the reason Amazon is directionally successful. Direction and true-over-null/wrong utility strengthen, yet base erosion strengthens more and net FULL ranking falls below QC and the two-event FULL. This directly separates correct history response from safe end-to-end integration.
interpretation.unused_headroom: The fixed-response oracle still exposes unused label-aligned headroom, but actual attribution is already far above random and directionally useful; headroom alone is therefore not a failure diagnosis.
interpretation.cross_dataset_rule: Amazon-C4 does not veto a pattern observed on other suitable datasets. It narrows prevalence and remains in the final evidence matrix as a boundary case rather than being discarded.

unsupported: The direction gap is universal across LLM4Rec datasets.
Amazon-C4 independently replicates the KuaiSearch strict-nonrepeat failure.
The Amazon wrong-user contrast proves same-query user provenance.
Base degradation and response-direction failure are the same mechanism.
next_action: Complete the pre-outcome-selected JDsearch v3 functional replication. Use it to decide whether the primary cross-source motivation is nonrepeat response-direction conversion, base/history entanglement, or a scoped combination; do not formulate architecture before that decision.
